stem: A ecology study surveys 15 sampled plots and observes 3 positive outcomes out of 15 (20.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (5 observations, rate = 20.0%) and Group B (10 observations, rate = 20.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 37% and 53% of the population. Estimate the true species occurrence rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.1793 (range [0.1756, 0.183]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0705, 0.4519]. Point estimate: 0.1793.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.45: 0.45×0.154 + 0.55×0.2 = 0.1793.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
